title: caltha palustris var. major
intro: Caltha palustris var. major, also known as the large marsh marigold, is a perennial plant in the buttercup family that grows in wetlands and marshy areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and fever. The health benefits of Caltha palustris var. major include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for its medicinal uses, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
